title: mallotus tiliifolius
intro: Mallotus tiliifolius is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the 'Indian varnish tree' due to its glossy bark. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Mallotus tiliifolius include its potential in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. In herbal preparations, Mallotus tiliifolius is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make a paste from its roots, which is applied topically to treat skin conditions.
